questions.question: Choose the modal verb that expresses definite intention or certainty in a hypothetical result.
questions.prompt: If I found a wallet on the street, I _____ return it to the owner immediately.
questions.explanation: Use 'would' when expressing a certain or definite result in imaginary conditional sentences. 'Could' and 'might' suggest only possibility or ability, not certainty.

questions.arabicHint: القدرة في الحالة الرئيسية (Could = would be able to)
questions.question: Select the verb that expresses hypothetical ability rather than definite intention.
questions.prompt: If I had wings, I _____ fly like a bird across the ocean.
questions.explanation: In the main clause, 'could' means 'would be able to,' indicating hypothetical ability. 'Would' would imply a definite action, while 'could' implies capability.

questions.question: Choose the verb that expresses possibility rather than certainty about a hypothetical outcome.
questions.prompt: If I saved enough money, I _____ buy a new phone or I might keep the old one.
questions.explanation: 'Might' indicates possibility (perhaps yes, perhaps no), whereas 'would' indicates a definite hypothetical result.

questions.arabicHint: الأمنية في الحاضر: Wish + Past Simple
questions.question: Complete the sentence to express a wish about a present situation.
questions.prompt: I don't have my own room. I wish I _____ a private space to study.
questions.explanation: Use 'wish' + past simple to express a desire for a present situation to be different. Do not use 'would' when the subject of wish and the subject of the verb are the same.

questions.question: Choose the correct form to express a wish about lacking ability in the present.
questions.prompt: I can't drive legally yet. I wish I _____ take the car to school.
questions.explanation: Use 'wish' + 'could' when regretting that you don't have the ability to do something in the present.

questions.question: Select the verb used to wish for a change in someone else's behavior or a future situation.
questions.prompt: My roommate never cleans the kitchen. I wish he _____ help with the housework.
questions.explanation: Use 'wish' + 'would' to express desire for someone else to change their behavior or for a future situation to be different.

questions.question: Choose the correct verb for the if-clause to express hypothetical ability in the condition itself.
questions.prompt: _____ speak three languages fluently, I would work as a tour guide.
questions.explanation: 'Could' in the if-clause means 'if someone were able to.' It describes hypothetical ability in the condition, not the result.

questions.arabicHint: were لجميع الضمائر في الإنجليزية الرسمية
questions.question: Select the grammatically correct form for formal English in imaginary conditionals.
questions.prompt: If I _____ the principal, I would change the school schedule.
questions.explanation: In formal English, use 'were' for all subjects (I/he/she/it) in imaginary conditionals (subjunctive mood), though 'was' is accepted in informal speech.

questions.question: Complete the negative wish about a current unwanted situation.
questions.prompt: I have to work tomorrow morning. I wish I _____ have to wake up so early.
questions.explanation: For negative wishes about present facts, use 'wish' + past simple negative (didn't). 'Wouldn't' is used for future/behavior wishes, not present facts.

questions.question: Choose the verb that expresses definite intention, not merely hypothetical ability.
questions.prompt: If we won the competition, we _____ celebrate at the best restaurant for sure.
questions.explanation: 'Would' expresses definite intention or certainty about a hypothetical action. 'Could' only indicates that the action is possible or that you have the ability to do it.

questions.question: Select the verb that indicates possibility among several options, not definite intention.
questions.prompt: If I met a famous actor, I _____ ask for an autograph, or I might just take a photo.
questions.explanation: 'Might' indicates that something is only one possible option. 'Would' would imply a definite planned action in this hypothetical scenario.

questions.question: Select the correct contraction used in imaginary conditional sentences.
questions.prompt: If I lived near the beach, _____ go swimming every weekend.
questions.explanation: 'I'd' is the contraction of 'I would.' Contractions are commonly used in both spoken and written imaginary conditionals with all subjects.

questions.arabicHint: Could بمعنى القدرة على النتيجة (would be able to)
questions.question: Choose the verb that specifically means 'would be able to' in this hypothetical result.
questions.prompt: If we practiced harder, we _____ beat the champion team.
questions.explanation: Here 'could' means 'would be able to' - it expresses that the hypothetical condition would give us the capability to achieve this result, whereas 'would' simply predicts the result itself.

questions.arabicHint: was في اللغة العامية مع الضمير I
questions.question: Choose the form commonly used in informal spoken English with 'I wish'.
questions.prompt: I wish I _____ taller so I could reach the top shelf.
questions.explanation: While 'were' is formally correct for all subjects, 'was' is commonly used with 'I' in informal spoken English when expressing wishes about the present.

questions.question: Select the correct inverted form that omits 'if' in this formal conditional sentence.
questions.prompt: _____ in your position, I would accept the scholarship immediately.
questions.explanation: In very formal English, we can omit 'if' and invert the subject and 'were' (Were I = If I were). This inversion is not used with 'was.'

questions.arabicHint: الاسم المعدود: يمكن العد وله مفرد وجمع
questions.question: Identify which noun is count and can be used with both 'a' and a plural form.
questions.prompt: Which of these is a count noun?
questions.explanation: Count nouns like 'coin' can be counted (a coin, two coins). Noncount nouns like money, information, and advice cannot be counted and have no plural form.

questions.arabicHint: الاسم غير المعدود: لا يمكن العد ولا يستخدم معه a/an
questions.question: Select the noncount noun that cannot be used with 'a' or made plural.
questions.prompt: Which word is a noncount noun?
questions.explanation: Rice is a noncount noun. We cannot say 'a rice' or 'rices.' We must say 'some rice' or 'a bowl of rice.' The other options are count nouns with singular and plural forms.

questions.question: Choose the correct quantity word for this affirmative statement.
questions.prompt: There is _____ pollution in big cities.
questions.explanation: We use 'some' in affirmative statements with noncount nouns (pollution) and plural nouns. 'Any' is used for negatives and questions.

questions.question: Select the correct word for this negative sentence.
questions.prompt: There aren't _____ sport facilities in this neighborhood.
questions.explanation: In negative statements, we use 'any' with plural and noncount nouns. 'No' would create a double negative with 'not' or 'n't.

questions.question: Choose the correct word to complete this question.
questions.prompt: Is there _____ crime in your area?
questions.explanation: We use 'any' in questions with plural and noncount nouns. Use 'some' only in questions when offering something or expecting a 'yes' answer.

questions.arabicHint: No لإعطاء معنى النفي في جملة مثبتة = not any
questions.question: Select the phrase that uses 'no' to give a negative meaning in an affirmative sentence.
questions.prompt: There _____ recycling facilities at the school last year.
questions.explanation: 'No' + noun in an affirmative sentence gives a negative meaning equal to 'not any.' Since 'facilities' is plural, we use 'are no' (There are no facilities = There aren't any facilities).

questions.arabicHint: الاسم غير المعدود يأخذ فعلاً مفرداً
questions.question: Choose the correct singular verb to agree with this noncount noun.
questions.prompt: The information about prices _____ very helpful.
questions.explanation: Noncount nouns like 'information' (and news, advice, money) always take a singular verb, even though they may end in -s or refer to many things.

questions.question: Select the correct question phrase for this plural count noun.
questions.prompt: _____ Saudi riyals does this thobe cost?
questions.explanation: Use 'How many' with plural count nouns (riyals) that can be counted individually. Use 'How much' with noncount nouns.

questions.question: Choose the correct question phrase for this noncount noun.
questions.prompt: _____ money do you need to buy the gold bracelet?
questions.explanation: Use 'How much' with noncount nouns (money) that cannot be counted individually. Use 'How many' only with plural count nouns.

questions.arabicHint: الأسماء غير المعدودة لا تأتي مع a/an ولا تأخذ s
questions.question: Identify the grammatically correct form of this noncount noun.
questions.prompt: Can you give me _____ about good stores?
questions.explanation: Advice is a noncount noun. It cannot use 'a/an' and has no plural form (-s). We can say 'some advice' or just 'advice,' but never 'an advice' or 'advices.'

questions.arabicHint: جمع الاسم المعدود المنتهي بـ sh (يضاف es)
questions.question: Select the correct plural form of this count noun.
questions.prompt: If I had three _____, I would buy a car, a house, and travel the world.
questions.explanation: Count nouns ending in -sh, -ch, -s, -x, or -z add -es to form the plural (wish → wishes), not just -s.

questions.arabicHint: Some في العروض والأسئلة التي نتوقع الإيجاب فيها
questions.question: Choose the correct word for this offer expecting a positive response.
questions.prompt: Would you like _____ tea with your dates?
questions.explanation: Although this is a question, we use 'some' (not 'any') in polite offers and requests when we expect the answer might be yes.

questions.arabicHint: There is مع المفرد و There are مع الجمع
questions.question: Select the correct 'there' structure for this plural noun.
questions.prompt: _____ some excellent discounts on cotton thobes today.
questions.explanation: Use 'There are' with plural count nouns (discounts). Use 'There is' with singular count nouns and all noncount nouns.

questions.arabicHint: توافق الفعل مع المفرد والجمع في الأسئلة عن السعر
questions.question: Choose the correct verb form for this shopping question about a singular item.
questions.prompt: How much _____ this wool sweater?
questions.explanation: With singular count nouns (sweater), use 'is' in price questions. Use 'are' with plural nouns (How much are these sandals?).

questions.arabicHint: الفرق بين استخدام No و Not any
questions.question: Identify which sentence correctly transforms the negative meaning using 'no' instead of 'not any'.
questions.prompt: Which sentence means the same as 'There isn't any cash in my wallet'?
options: There is no cash in my wallet.
There are no cash in my wallet.
There is some cash in my wallet.
There are any cash in my wallet.
questions.answer: There is no cash in my wallet.
questions.explanation: 'No' + noun gives a negative meaning in an affirmative sentence structure. 'There is no cash' = 'There isn't any cash.' Since 'cash' is noncount (singular), we use 'is no.'

questions.question: Complete the wish expressing regret about lacking a present ability.
questions.prompt: I don't know how to swim. I wish I _____ dive into the pool.
questions.explanation: Use 'wish' + 'could' to express regret about not having a specific ability right now. 'Would' is used for future changes or others' behavior, not your own abilities.

questions.arabicHint: القدرة في النتيجة (Could = would be able to)
questions.question: Choose the modal that expresses hypothetical ability rather than definite intention.
questions.prompt: If I had a library card, I _____ borrow books every weekend.
questions.explanation: 'Could' in the main clause means 'would be able to.' It indicates that the condition would give you the capability, whereas 'would' suggests you definitely would perform the action.

questions.question: Express a wish about a present situation that you want to be different.
questions.prompt: I live far from school. I wish I _____ closer to the campus.
questions.explanation: Use 'wish' + past simple to express desire for a present situation to be different. Do not use 'would' when the subject of 'wish' and the verb are the same person.

questions.question: Select the verb that expresses possibility among several options, not definite intention.
questions.prompt: If I won a trip to Europe, I _____ visit Paris first, or I might go to Rome.
questions.explanation: 'Might' indicates this is only one possible choice. Using 'would' would imply a definite decision to visit Paris, excluding other options.

questions.question: Choose the correct verb for the if-clause to express hypothetical ability.
questions.prompt: _____ afford a new phone, I would buy the latest model.
questions.explanation: 'Could' in the if-clause means 'if someone were able to.' It establishes the hypothetical ability that must exist for the result to happen.

questions.question: Select the correct form to wish for a change in another person's behavior.
questions.prompt: My brother never wakes up for Fajr prayer. I wish he _____ get up early.
questions.explanation: Use 'wish' + 'would' when you want someone else to change their behavior or habits. 'Could' would only mean you want him to have the ability, not the willingness.

questions.question: Complete the sentence with the correct contraction for the hypothetical result.
questions.prompt: If I saw a sale at the mall, _____ buy new shoes immediately.
questions.explanation: 'I'd' is the contraction of 'I would,' which is used in the main clause to show the definite hypothetical result of the condition.

questions.question: Choose the formal inverted structure that omits 'if' entirely.
questions.prompt: _____ in your position, I would accept that job offer.
questions.explanation: In very formal English, omit 'if' and invert: 'Were I' = 'If I were.' This inversion only works with 'were,' not with 'was.'

questions.arabicHint: الأمنية السلبية للضرورة (Wish + didn't have to)
questions.question: Complete the wish about wanting to avoid a present obligation.
questions.prompt: I have to study for exams tonight. I wish I _____ to study so hard.
questions.explanation: For present obligations you wish didn't exist, use 'wish' + past simple negative (didn't have to). 'Wouldn't have' is used for future refusals, not present facts.

questions.question: Select the modal expressing definite hypothetical intention, not merely ability.
questions.prompt: If I became class president, I _____ organize more sports activities for sure.
questions.explanation: 'Would' expresses a definite plan or intention in the hypothetical scenario. 'Could' only indicates you would possess the ability to organize them.

questions.question: Choose the polite and appropriate response to the shop assistant.
questions.prompt: Assistant: 'Can I help you, sir?' Customer: '_____'
options: Yes, please. I'm looking for a cotton thobe.
questions.answer: Yes, please. I'm looking for a cotton thobe.
questions.explanation: The standard polite response is 'Yes, please' followed by stating what you're looking for. Direct negative responses are considered rude.

questions.question: Select the correct question word to ask about clothing material.
questions.prompt: Customer: '_____' Assistant: 'They are made from wool.'
options: What are these pants made from?
Where are these pants made from?
Who are these pants made from?
When are these pants made from?
questions.answer: What are these pants made from?
questions.explanation: Use 'What... made from?' to ask about materials. 'Made from' is used when the original material has been transformed (like wool into fabric).

questions.arabicHint: How much مع المفرد / How many مع الجمع
questions.question: Choose the correct question form for this singular clothing item.
questions.prompt: Customer: '_____' Assistant: 'It's 209 riyals.'
questions.explanation: Use 'How much is' with singular count nouns (dress) and noncount nouns. Use 'How much are' only with plural nouns.

questions.arabicHint: Some في العروض المتوقع فيها الإيجاب
questions.question: Select the correct word for this polite offer expecting a positive response.
questions.prompt: Shop assistant: 'Would you like _____ tea while you browse?'
questions.explanation: In polite offers where we expect the answer might be yes, we use 'some' rather than 'any,' even though it is grammatically a question.

questions.arabicHint: الأسماء غير المعدودة مع الفعل المفرد
questions.question: Choose the correct singular verb to agree with this noncount noun.
questions.prompt: The advice my counselor gave me _____ very helpful.
questions.explanation: Noncount nouns like 'advice,' 'information,' 'news,' and 'money' always take singular verbs, even though they may end in -s or seem plural in meaning.

questions.arabicHint: جمع الاسم المعدود المنتهي بـ z
questions.question: Select the correct plural form to use with a number.
questions.prompt: The competition awards four _____ to the best students.
questions.explanation: Regular count nouns add -s or -es to form the plural. 'Prize' simply adds -s (prizes). The plural form is required after numbers greater than one.

questions.question: Rewrite the sentence using 'no' to maintain the same negative meaning.
questions.prompt: There isn't any discount on gold bracelets. = There _____ discount on gold bracelets.
questions.explanation: 'There is no' = 'There isn't any.' Since 'discount' is singular in this context, we use 'is no.' Avoid double negatives like 'isn't no.'

questions.question: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence about reduced prices.
questions.prompt: This store offers a 15% _____ on all sandals today.
questions.explanation: A 'discount' is a specific reduction in price (e.g., 15% off). We say 'a 15% discount,' not 'a 15% sale.'

questions.question: Select the natural question asking about clothing size.
questions.prompt: Shop assistant: '_____' Customer: 'I'd like to try a large.'
questions.explanation: In shopping contexts for clothes, 'What size are you?' is the standard question. Sizes refer to small, medium, large, etc.

questions.arabicHint: Any في السؤال الحقيقي (لا نعرف الجواب)
questions.question: Choose the correct word for this genuine question where the answer is unknown.
questions.prompt: Do you have _____ leather shoes in size 42?
questions.explanation: In real questions where we don't know if the answer is yes or no, use 'any.' Reserve 'some' for offers and requests where we expect or hope for a 'yes' answer.

questions.prompt: If you were stranded on a _____, you would have no neighbors or shops nearby.
questions.explanation: A desert island is a small remote island with no people living on it, perfect for the hypothetical scenario of complete isolation.

questions.prompt: Even when studying abroad, it is important to _____ with your family back home.
questions.explanation: To keep in touch means to continue communicating with someone regularly so you do not lose contact with them.

questions.arabicHint: لقاء عن قرب (غالباً مع كائن غريب)
questions.prompt: The science fiction movie showed a _____ between the astronaut and an alien creature.
questions.explanation: A close encounter means meeting someone or something unexpectedly, often used to describe meetings with extraterrestrial beings.

questions.prompt: An _____ is a being that comes from a planet other than Earth.
questions.explanation: Extraterrestrial refers to any creature or life form originating from outside planet Earth.

questions.prompt: The weather in that tropical country stays warm _____.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'whole year round' means continuously throughout all twelve months of the year without seasonal changes.

questions.prompt: The view from the mountain top was absolutely _____.
questions.explanation: Fabulous means extremely good, wonderful, or amazing. It is often used in informal contexts to show enthusiasm.

questions.prompt: The two winners decided to _____ the cash prize equally between them.
questions.explanation: To split something means to divide it into separate parts or shares, often between two or more people.

questions.prompt: Many students admire Nelson Mandela as a _____ who changed history.
questions.explanation: A historical figure is a famous person who lived in the past and had an important impact on history.

questions.prompt: The Roman _____ controlled large parts of Europe and Asia long ago.
questions.explanation: An empire is a large group of countries or territories ruled by a single leader or powerful government.

questions.prompt: The charity works hard to fight _____ in poor communities.
questions.explanation: Poverty is the state of being extremely poor, without enough money or resources to meet basic needs.

questions.prompt: A _____ is someone who donates large amounts of money to help others and support good causes.
questions.explanation: A philanthropist is a wealthy person who uses their money to help those in need and support charitable organizations.

questions.prompt: The scientist received a prestigious _____ for his discovery in medicine.
questions.explanation: An award is a prize or honor given to recognize someone's outstanding achievements in a particular field.

questions.prompt: The Nobel Prize _____ gave speeches about their important research.
questions.explanation: Laureates are people who have been awarded a prestigious prize or honor, particularly in academic, scientific, or artistic fields.

questions.prompt: The champion received a gold _____ to wear around his neck during the ceremony.
questions.explanation: A medallion is a large medal, usually decorative and made of precious metal, worn around the neck as a symbol of honor.

questions.prompt: Students' opinions about the best sport _____; some like football, while others prefer basketball.
questions.explanation: To vary means to be different or to change from one situation to another. If opinions vary, people have different ideas about something.

questions.prompt: Living far from family and friends can make a person feel _____ sometimes.
questions.explanation: Lonely describes the sad feeling of being alone and wishing for company or friends nearby.

questions.prompt: This new medicine will _____ humanity by curing a dangerous disease.
questions.explanation: To benefit means to help someone or to give them an advantage. When something benefits mankind, it helps all people.

questions.prompt: The invention of electricity was a great gift to _____.
questions.explanation: Mankind refers to all human beings collectively, considered as a single group or species.

questions.prompt: His _____ with a famous writer at the airport was completely unexpected.
questions.explanation: An encounter is a meeting, especially one that happens by chance or unexpectedly rather than being planned in advance.

questions.prompt: Modern _____ like smartphones have changed how we communicate daily.
questions.explanation: Technologies refer to modern scientific methods, skills, and devices used in industry or daily life.

questions.prompt: The cash _____ for the competition winner was 750,000 riyals.
questions.explanation: A prize is money or something valuable given to the winner of a competition. Do not confuse with 'price,' which is what you pay to buy something.

questions.prompt: Old classmates often use social media to _____ with each other.
questions.explanation: The phrasal verb 'keep in touch' means to maintain communication with someone so that you continue to know what is happening in their life.

questions.prompt: Winners of the academic competition received a handwritten _____ proving their achievement.
questions.explanation: A certificate is an official document stating that you have completed a course or achieved something noteworthy.

questions.prompt: The two Egyptian scholars decided to _____ the award for their joint research project.
questions.explanation: To share means to divide something between two or more people so that each person gets a portion of it.

questions.prompt: Many people wish they could _____ their appearance, but Hameed is happy the way he is.
questions.explanation: To change means to make something different. Some people would change how they look if they could, while others are satisfied with their current appearance.

questions.prompt: Which material comes from sheep and keeps you warm in winter?
questions.explanation: Wool is a natural fiber obtained from sheep and is commonly used to make warm clothing like sweaters.

questions.prompt: Expensive shoes and bags are often made of _____, which comes from animal skin.
questions.explanation: Leather is a durable material made from the skin of animals, typically cows, used for shoes, bags, and jackets.

questions.prompt: A wedding ring is usually made of _____ or silver because these metals are valuable and do not rust.
questions.explanation: Gold is a precious yellow metal used to make expensive jewelry like bracelets and rings. It is often paired with silver in the options.

questions.prompt: Many Saudi men wear a white _____ to work or special occasions.
questions.explanation: A thobe is the traditional long white garment worn by men in Saudi Arabia and other Gulf countries.

questions.prompt: People wear _____ on their feet in hot weather to stay cool.
questions.explanation: Sandals are open shoes that expose most of the foot, making them ideal for hot weather.

questions.prompt: She received a beautiful silver _____ for her birthday that she wears on her wrist.
questions.explanation: A bracelet is a piece of jewelry worn around the wrist, often made of metal like gold or silver.

questions.prompt: If I won a million riyals, I would _____ the money into three parts: one for family, one for travel, and one for savings.
questions.explanation: To divide means to separate something into parts or portions. You divide money when you split it among different uses or people.

questions.prompt: The winner of the competition received a large _____ of money as a reward.
questions.explanation: Cash refers to money in the form of coins or notes. A 'cash prize' is money awarded to the winner of a game or competition.

questions.prompt: Before traveling, you should _____ your trip carefully to avoid problems.
questions.explanation: To plan out means to organize or arrange all the details of something in advance. If you have something 'all planned out,' you know exactly what you will do.

questions.prompt: At the wedding party, everyone danced and laughed all night; they really _____!
questions.explanation: The idiom 'have a ball' means to have a very enjoyable time or to enjoy yourself very much.

questions.prompt: If you could choose any _____ for your vacation, would you pick the beach or the mountains?
questions.explanation: A destination is the place where someone or something is going. When you travel, your destination is the end point of your journey.

questions.prompt: _____, the best way to learn English is to practice speaking every day.
questions.explanation: The phrase 'to me' is used to introduce a personal opinion. It means 'in my opinion' or 'from my point of view.'

questions.prompt: You need more work _____ before you can apply for a manager position.
questions.explanation: Experience is the knowledge or skill you get from doing a job or activity over a period of time.

questions.prompt: Buses and trains are examples of _____ that help people move around the city.
questions.explanation: Public transportation refers to shared vehicles like buses and trains that transport many people along set routes.

questions.prompt: The city needs more _____ to process plastic and paper waste properly.
questions.explanation: Recycling facilities are special centers or plants where waste materials are processed to be reused instead of being thrown away.

questions.prompt: Factories and cars cause air _____ that makes the sky dirty and harms health.
questions.explanation: Pollution is the process of making air, water, or land dirty and dangerous, usually with chemicals or waste.

questions.prompt: Our neighborhood needs more _____ like football fields and swimming pools.
questions.explanation: Sport facilities are places and equipment designed for athletic activities and exercise, such as stadiums, gyms, and courts.

questions.prompt: Many young people cannot afford to buy a house because of _____ prices in big cities.
questions.explanation: Housing refers to homes and places to live. When housing is expensive, it costs a lot of money to rent or buy a home.

questions.prompt: In the supermarket, you push a _____ to carry your items before paying.
questions.explanation: A shopping cart is a wheeled basket used in supermarkets to hold groceries while customers select items from the shelves.

questions.prompt: Be careful with coffee; if you spill it on your white shirt, it will leave an ugly brown _____.
questions.explanation: A stain is a mark or spot that is difficult to remove, often caused by spilling food, drink, or other substances on fabric.

questions.prompt: It is wrong to _____ by looking at your friend's paper during a test.
questions.explanation: To cheat means to act dishonestly to gain an advantage, such as copying answers during an exam instead of doing your own work.

questions.prompt: When you borrow a book from the library, you must _____ it on time.
questions.explanation: To return means to give something back to the person or place you borrowed it from.

questions.prompt: The president decides how much _____ people must pay to the government.
questions.explanation: Taxes are amounts of money that citizens and businesses must pay to the government to fund public services.

questions.prompt: I met _____ at the mall yesterday who told me an amazing story.
questions.explanation: In informal English, 'this guy' is a casual way to refer to 'this person' or 'this man' when telling a story.

questions.question: Identify the echo question used to express disbelief or confirm what was heard.
questions.prompt: A: I wouldn't know what to do with a million dollars. B: _____?
options: You wouldn't know what to do with so much money
questions.answer: You wouldn't know what to do with so much money
questions.explanation: An echo question repeats part of what someone said to show surprise or disbelief. Here, repeating 'You wouldn't know...?' shows shock at the statement.
